compositional_truth_check.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: Pot is present but contains only soil/debris.
compositional_truth_check.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot appears empty.
compositional_truth_check.P3_Pothos: Present: Single leaf remains, anchored by white rabbit.
compositional_truth_check.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss: No evidence of specimen.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The biome is largely non-viable. Only one Pothos leaf remains from the original registry.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, potentially over-saturated; no signs of active growth.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3; paper receipt introduced in T-1/Current.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, though soil surface appears stagnant.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Progressive loss of structural integrity in P3 leaf; P1/P2/P4 remained dormant/empty.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Introduction of paper debris; P3 leaf shows no recovery.

visual_health_inference.P3_Pothos: Terminal decline; the single leaf is the only remnant of a failed propagation or collapse.
visual_health_inference.General_Biome: Non-functional; high probability of root rot or total desiccation of root systems.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of advanced collapse. The registry is largely invalidated by the absence of the primary specimens. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor, though it is in a state of extreme stress. The presence of eggshells suggests an intervention that failed to prevent the systemic loss of the other specimens. The environment is currently more of a soil-holding vessel than a living botanical collection.
